questions.question: In which year did Bangladesh gain independence?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh gained independence on December 16, 1971, after the Liberation War.

questions.question: Who was the first President of Bangladesh?
questions.explanation: Abu Sayeed Chowdhury was the first President of Bangladesh from 1971 to 1973.

questions.question: The ancient civilization of Mesopotamia was located between which two rivers?
questions.explanation: Mesopotamia, known as the cradle of civilization, was located between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers.

questions.question: Who was known as the 'Father of the Nation' of Bangladesh?
questions.explanation: Sheikh Mujibur Rahman is known as the 'Father of the Nation' (Jatir Pita) of Bangladesh.

questions.question: The Mughal Empire was founded by:
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in India in 1526 after the Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: World War II ended in which year?
questions.explanation: World War II ended in 1945 with the surrender of Japan in August.

questions.question: The Language Movement in Bangladesh took place in which year?
questions.explanation: The Language Movement occurred in 1952, particularly on February 21, now observed as International Mother Language Day.

questions.question: Who built the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.question: The Battle of Plassey was fought in:
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey was fought on June 23, 1757, marking the beginning of British rule in India.

questions.question: Which ancient wonder of the world was located in Alexandria?
questions.explanation: The Lighthouse of Alexandria (Pharos of Alexandria) was one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.
